summary: A natural-language goal no longer becomes a blindly approvable generic Nori Contract: generic draft output carries a discovery-required acceptance basis, and nori-acceptance instructs agents to show ACCEPTANCE-BASIS questions before asking for approval.
reviewability: Inspect the nori-acceptance Skill and run tests covering generic draft review findings.
limitations: This does not generate a fully specific final contract by itself; it prevents premature approval and routes the agent back to discovery questions.
